name: Stir-Fried Spiced Pork on Couscous
recipeIngredient: 1 lb Boneless pork loin or shoulder (fat trimmed, cut into 1/2 x 3" strips)
recipeInstructions:
- Fat trimmed, cut into 1/2 x 3" strips
1. Mix cabbage with vinegar; set aside.
2. In a 2-quart pan, bring broth to a boil. Stir in the couscous, cover pan tightly, remove from heat, and let stand until couscous is tender to bite and most of the liquid is absorbed, about 5 minutes.
3. Meanwhile, place a wok or 10-12" frying pan over high heat. When pan is hot, add 2 tablespoons oil and half the pork; stir-fry until pork is lightly browned, about 3 minutes. Remove from pan; to pan, add 2 tablespoons oil and remaining pork and repeat stir-frying step.
4. Add to cooked meat: 2 more tablespoons oil, onion, ginger, and garlic; stir-fry for 2 minutes. Return pork to pan, and add sauce. Stir until sauce boils, about 2 minutes.
5. Place cabbage in a layer on a large platter or divide among 4 dinner plates. Mound hot couscous on cabbage. Spoon pork and sauce over couscous. Sprinkle with mint.
SAUCE
Stir together chicken broth, orange juice, cornstarch, soy sauce, coriander, cumin, and cayenne.
